emails.subject: Golf for Sarah
emails.body: Golf for Sarah Kay: Here is the information on the charity golf outing we discussed. Check out sarahssoldiers.com for more information on Sarah and the efforts to help her. Attached at the bottom of this email is a pledge form. Thanks for your support in this good cause. Fred Forwarded by Fred Mitro/HOU/ECT on 04/12/2001 03:54 PM Stephen Morse@ENRON COMMUNICATIONS on 04/12/2001 03:00:16 PM To: Enron Golfers: Hey guys, Sarah Edwards is a friend of a friend's daughter who desperately needs help to stay alive and all you duffers out there can help: The 100 holes for golf for Sarah Edwards will be Monday June 4 at Sweetwater CC. Sarah needs a lung transplant and approximately $150,000 of the costs are not covered by insurance or medicaid. For more info on Sarah, visit Sarahssoldiers.com Here is how the 100 holes for golf works: *Free golf at Sweetwater *You play as many holes as you can (100 is not mandatory) We provide your lunch, drinks and snacks You get pledges from your friends to play *pledges may be flat amounts, per hole, per birdie or per eagle Lets all keep Sarah alive! Can I get some other tentative commitments? thanks! Steve Steve Morse Enron Broadband Services 713-853-7137-work 713-569-7912-cell email: stephen_morse@enron.net pledge.doc

emails.subject: Re: Golf for Sarah
emails.body: Re: Golf for Sarah I can't open the form, but I'm good for $50. Is this something Enron can match? From: Fred Mitro/ENRON@enronXgate on 06/01/2001 01:34 PM

emails.subject: RE: Golf for Sarah
emails.body: RE: Golf for Sarah Thanks for your support. We checked into the matching, but unfortunately since the group raising this money was not formed as a Charitable Organization under the tax reg's, the ability to get Enron to match is not availible. The goal of each golfer is to raise at least $1000 towards the $150K goal, and your donation puts me over the $1K bar. Thanks again. Fred
